themes: model competition (GPT-5.5, Grok Build, Codex continuation)

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Concrete measurement: Opus 4.7 uses 1.46x tokens for text and up to 3x for images at the same per-token price — the most credible cost data in the feed.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Important correction to the above: the 3x image cost only applies at higher resolutions, so small images are effectively the same price as 4.6.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Field report of Opus 4.7 confidently sticking to wrong answers and forcing a rollback to 4.6 — directly relevant to AI product strategy decisions about model selection.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick's counterfactual — OpenAI could have hidden reasoners and skipped DeepSeek — is a sharp strategic framing worth absorbing for anyone thinking about AI competitive dynamics.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: High-signal indie dev take: if your roadmap exceeds 30 days in the AI era, you're not moving fast enough — 87K views signals this resonated widely.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Argues every human system (finance, comms, time management) must be rebuilt from first principles for agents — useful framing for agent-native product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: 30-min workshop from Claude Code's creator on vibe-coding — highest repost count in the feed among tooling posts, likely the most actionable Claude Code content here.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Manifest is an open-source model router that picks cheap vs. expensive models per request in under 2ms, claiming 70% cost reduction for agent builders — worth evaluating for cost control.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: Anthropic's official 33-page Claude Skills guide surfaced here with 625K views — if you haven't read it, this is the pointer.

voices.why: Only voice in the feed doing actual empirical measurement on Opus 4.7 token costs rather than speculating — directly actionable for API cost planning.
Consistently sharp on agent-native product strategy and distribution; the 30-day roadmap post alone got 87K views and reflects real indie dev instincts.
Provides clean strategic framing on AI competitive dynamics (the reasoner history thread) with academic rigor and no hype.

examples: @aakashgupta spamming 4x daily newsletter CTAs and off-topic lifestyle content (bones, tennis longevity, NASA, Nolan film)
@RoundtableSpace crypto/trading bot content (AAVE exploit, Polymarket bots, weather trader)
engagement-bait filler posts (GM CT, LOCK TF IN, WHAT ARE WE APING IN)

emerging.theme: model competition (GPT-5.5, Grok Build, Codex continuation)
